Spotlight: Fair winning 2m hurdler who wasn't discredited when fifth in 2m5f handicap at Market Rasen in May; much respected now going chasing.

RaceName: Worcestershire Ambassadors Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Much improved for Tom Lacey last season when a three-time 2m hurdles winner; off six months and made a promising start in this sphere with third of five in 2m1f handicap at Stratford 32 days ago, running on late under a patient ride; one to be interested in off a 2lb lower mark.

RaceName: Worcestershire Ambassadors Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Cut little ice in a handful of runs over hurdles but this Irish point runner-up was much improved on his chasing debut when a 28-1 winner of nine-runner handicap over C&D two weeks ago; up 5lb but open to progress, so he's a player.

RaceName: Worcestershire Ambassadors Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Ended his time over hurdles for Dan Skelton under something of a cloud; not disgraced on yard/chasing debut with third in 2m4f handicap here but pulled up in 2m7f handicap chase back here 20 days ago; has something to prove now.

RaceName: Worcestershire Ambassadors Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Made light of a five-month absence when gaining a third chasing win in his last four runs in 2m Warwick handicap in May; up 5lb but not taken lightly.

RaceName: Worcestershire Ambassadors Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Gained a first chasing success at Doncaster (2m) last February; infrequently raced since and he came in a well-held fourth of five in 2m5f Fakenham handicap 31 days ago; needs to take a big step forward.

RaceName: Worcestershire Ambassadors Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)
OwnerName: Martyn & Elaine Booth & The Maiden Over
Spotlight: Fair maiden hurdler; yet to offer much in three runs sent chasing though, undergoing wind surgery before pulled up in 2m7f handicap chase here 25 days ago; blinkers and tongue strap go on here.

RaceName: Worcestershire Ambassadors Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Signed off for Charles and Adam Pogson with 2m5f Fakenham chasing win last May; has failed to complete on all his five runs for his current stable however, tried in cheekpieces when pulled up at Leicester (2m4f) in February last time.

RaceName: Jane Newman Financial Planning Ltd Maiden Hurdle (Arc Summer Novices' Brush Qual') (GBB) (Div I)
Spotlight: Quite useful on the Flat for the Crisfords over 1m4f on turf and at staying trips on AW; returned from a year off with an encouraging hurdle debut over C&D (good) in June for new yard, settling well and mostly jumping soundly behind two who had won on the Flat in France; looks sure to improve.

RaceName: Jane Newman Financial Planning Ltd Maiden Hurdle (Arc Summer Novices' Brush Qual') (GBB) (Div I)
Spotlight: Placed on good to soft in all three bumper starts but she dropped out after making the early running on hurdle debut at Fontwell (about 2m1f, good; 16-1) in May; handicaps look more likely than a maiden win.

RaceName: Jane Newman Financial Planning Ltd Maiden Hurdle (Arc Summer Novices' Brush Qual') (GBB) (Div I)
Spotlight: Returned from two years off to win a Ludlow bumper in 2025; up against it on first two hurdle starts but he ran better when third of eight finishers at Bangor (about 2m3f, good) in March and wasn't far away, though held, when falling at the last at Warwick since; in contention.

RaceName: Jane Newman Financial Planning Ltd Maiden Hurdle (Arc Summer Novices' Brush Qual') (GBB) (Div I)
Spotlight: Flat winner at 1m2f in May 2025; refused to race in May this year but he came nearest to a first hurdle win when close up in an ordinary handicap at Southwell (2m, good) three weeks ago; shouldn't be far away.

RaceName: Jane Newman Financial Planning Ltd Maiden Hurdle (Arc Summer Novices' Brush Qual') (GBB) (Div I)
Spotlight: Early promise in a bumper in May 2025; has hinted at some ability over hurdles and this is easier than when a remote fourth over C&D in June but he will be eligible for handicaps after today.

RaceName: Jane Newman Financial Planning Ltd Maiden Hurdle (Arc Summer Novices' Brush Qual') (GBB) (Div I)
Spotlight: Out of a half-sister to Group 3 and Listed winners; has run respectably in two of three bumpers, most recently behind two newcomers here in May on yard debut; others may be too sharp for him now he's hurdling.

RaceName: Jane Newman Financial Planning Ltd Maiden Hurdle (Arc Summer Novices' Brush Qual') (GBB) (Div I)
Spotlight: Runner-up in a Galway bumper; has run with credit against some good prospects in first two hurdle starts at Downpatrick and Cartmel (both 2m6f on good) in May but he was struck into when pulled up at Southwell since; has a part to play if this much shorter trip doesn't count against him.

RaceName: Jane Newman Financial Planning Ltd Maiden Hurdle (Arc Summer Novices' Brush Qual') (GBB) (Div I)
Spotlight: Bred to come into his own over further than 2m but he's shown plenty of pace at Exeter and Uttoxeter (both on good) in his last two bumpers, getting worn down only late on; likely contender now hurdling.

RaceName: Jane Newman Financial Planning Ltd Maiden Hurdle (Arc Summer Novices' Brush Qual') (GBB) (Div I)
Spotlight: 300-1, pulled up on belated debut over C&D last month.

RaceName: Jane Newman Financial Planning Ltd Maiden Hurdle (Arc Summer Novices' Brush Qual') (GBB) (Div II)
Spotlight: Nearly won a bumper here last summer and later close up in two runs over hurdles; no improvement when beaten 10l here 20 days ago but he did race freely after a break; entitled to be in the thick of it.

RaceName: Jane Newman Financial Planning Ltd Maiden Hurdle (Arc Summer Novices' Brush Qual') (GBB) (Div II)
Spotlight: 1-14 on the Flat, the win coming over about 1m3f at Yarmouth (good; fair form) in April; beaten 14l into second in a six-runner maiden hurdle at Huntingdon a month ago; likely to need more here.

RaceName: Jane Newman Financial Planning Ltd Maiden Hurdle (Arc Summer Novices' Brush Qual') (GBB) (Div II)
Spotlight: 0-6 on Flat for the Gosdens; not beaten all that far in two of his three races over hurdles, latterly at Bangor in this new headgear combination; should have some role to play.

RaceName: Jane Newman Financial Planning Ltd Maiden Hurdle (Arc Summer Novices' Brush Qual') (GBB) (Div II)
Spotlight: Winning pointer; runner-up for a second time over hurdles (0-6) when rallying over C&D 20 days ago; none too consistent and may ideally prefer further, but he boasts some of the best form among these.

RaceName: Jane Newman Financial Planning Ltd Maiden Hurdle (Arc Summer Novices' Brush Qual') (GBB) (Div II)
Spotlight: The winner of a weak good-ground bumper at Exeter; raced freely in his two runs over hurdles; weakening when falling in the first but then finished clear of the third behind a long odds-on winner at Warwick (2m, good) and that's something to build on.

RaceName: Jane Newman Financial Planning Ltd Maiden Hurdle (Arc Summer Novices' Brush Qual') (GBB) (Div II)
Spotlight: Only 1-12 in points and 50-1 when tailed off on hurdle debut at Uttoxeter.

RaceName: Jane Newman Financial Planning Ltd Maiden Hurdle (Arc Summer Novices' Brush Qual') (GBB) (Div II)
Spotlight: Half-brother to high-class jumper Any Second Now; beaten 20l in a bumper and even further in a maiden hurdle here two weeks ago when again easy to back; in a top yard but has no obvious form claims and looks one for further down the line.

RaceName: Jane Newman Financial Planning Ltd Maiden Hurdle (Arc Summer Novices' Brush Qual') (GBB) (Div II)
Spotlight: The winner of two from 11 points; can only be watched on this hurdling debut.

RaceName: Jane Newman Financial Planning Ltd Maiden Hurdle (Arc Summer Novices' Brush Qual') (GBB) (Div II)
Spotlight: Closely related to high-class 2m hurdler Labaik; had wind surgery prior to running in a bumper here when he faded late on (wore a tongue-tie); sent off one of the favourites that day and should be capable of better.

RaceName: Jane Newman Financial Planning Ltd Maiden Hurdle (Arc Summer Novices' Brush Qual') (GBB) (Div II)
Spotlight: Raced freely when tailed off in his first bumper; hooded for the second at Hereford (2m, good) and stayed on encouragingly from well off the pace to be about 15l off the winner; an unlikely winner of this but might be one to keep an eye on.

RaceName: Jane Newman Financial Planning Ltd Maiden Hurdle (Arc Summer Novices' Brush Qual') (GBB) (Div II)
Spotlight: Minor form under rules for Tom Lacey; has since gone 2-7 in points; others are much preferred.

RaceName: Warrens Hall Riding School Champions Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Winless since 2024 but he signed off for James Moffatt with a fair sixth of 13 in 2m1f Cartmel handicap in May; not out of things for his new handler.

RaceName: Warrens Hall Riding School Champions Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Useful bumper winner for Ben Brookhouse; brought along steadily for current yard before much improved on his handicap debut with second of 11 at Southwell (2m) 22 days ago, running on strongly late on having had a lot to do; big player with better to come.

RaceName: Warrens Hall Riding School Champions Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: A fair 1m2f scorer on the Flat who got off the mark in this sphere in 2m Stratford handicap last June; back from ten months off with a promising third of eight over C&D two weeks ago, finishing well having suffered a poor run from two out; very much one to consider.

RaceName: Warrens Hall Riding School Champions Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Quick to make his mark for James Owen with a pair of 2m handicap hurdle wins at Stratford and Plumpton last summer; off the track since a remote sixth at Warwick (2m) seven months ago though, so needs to hit the ground running again.

RaceName: Warrens Hall Riding School Champions Handicap Hurdle
OwnerName: Mr & Mrs R Newiss & Gavin Teversham
Spotlight: Scored at Uttoxeter (2m) last May but this veteran has not proved easy to catch right since, last of ten on the Flat at Salisbury 17 days ago.

RaceName: Warrens Hall Riding School Champions Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Gained a first win in this sphere in five-runner handicap at Fakenham (2m) when last seen in January; up 3lb but has gone well fresh, so not ruled out after a break.

RaceName: Warrens Hall Riding School Champions Handicap Hurdle
OwnerName: Mrs H Watson & Mrs Carol Lee
Spotlight: A fair 2m Flat winner for George Boughey; in the frame on all his four runs since sent hurdling, undergoing wind surgery before a solid fourth of eight on his handicap debut over C&D two weeks ago; considered.

RaceName: Warrens Hall Riding School Champions Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Fairly useful bumper scorer last July; yet to match that form in five runs over hurdles, going too freely after six months off when ninth of 12 in 2m Ffos Las handicap in May; should strip sharper here.

RaceName: Warrens Hall Riding School Champions Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 33-1 but easily best run on his first go in handicaps when landing 13-runner event at Southwell (2m) in May; up 4lb but this lightly raced 5yo is open to further progress.

RaceName: Bri Wealth Accumulation Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Multiple point winner who has raced just four times over hurdles and won a 2m3f Fontwell handicap in May (good); disappointing off this 5lb higher mark next time but that looked too bad to be true.

RaceName: Bri Wealth Accumulation Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Point/bumper winner; might be finding her feet over hurdles having stayed on encouragingly for third here in May and she looks ready for this longer distance.

RaceName: Bri Wealth Accumulation Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Placed in low-grade AW handicaps for Olly Murphy earlier this year; beaten 20l over hurdles on yard debut and then unseated in his first chase; risky.

RaceName: Bri Wealth Accumulation Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Third in both his handicaps and finished with a rattle in the latest at Perth (2m, good); on the back of that and raised in distance, he's firmly in the reckoning.

RaceName: Bri Wealth Accumulation Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Won a Class 6 handicap in April (1m4f, good) but tailed off in his next Flat race and suffered the same fate back over hurdles at Uttoxeter (first-time blinkers now removed); looks risky.

RaceName: Bri Wealth Accumulation Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Winning pointer with placed form over hurdles but he's now 0-14 and his runs earlier this year were poor.

RaceName: Bri Wealth Accumulation Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle
OwnerName: D S Dennis & John Hw Finch
Spotlight: Showed a tad more when beaten about 12l in his first handicap (2m3f, good; 33-1) but was still only fifth of the eight runners; dropped 4lb.

RaceName: Bri Wealth Accumulation Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Modest profile under rules but handicaps were always going to be more like it and he needs checking in the betting; raised in distance.

RaceName: Bri Wealth Accumulation Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 0-2 in bumpers and 0-7 over hurdles; 20-1 when never a factor in a C&D handicap 20 days ago.

RaceName: Bri Wealth Accumulation Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Ropey form figures for previous yard and well behind in two runs for this one; tackles her first handicap but with much to prove.

RaceName: Bri Wealth Accumulation Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 1m3f winner two years ago; 0-4 over hurdles and pretty much tailed off in two handicaps (2m4f/2m); has an awful lot to prove.

RaceName: Britannia Dynamic Logistics Promises Delivered Conditional Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Bagged a hat-trick over fences (up to 2m2f) in France last summer; yet to match that form for his new yard but better signs with fifth of 11 over C&D 25 days ago; not out of things if building on it.

RaceName: Britannia Dynamic Logistics Promises Delivered Conditional Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: On a losing run but he got back on track with second of eight in 2m7f handicap at Bangor five days ago; now needs to back it up.

RaceName: Britannia Dynamic Logistics Promises Delivered Conditional Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: A three-time chase winner (at up to 3m2f) last season; pulled up in 2m7f handicap chase here in May, so has it to prove on his first run in this sphere since April 2024.

RaceName: Britannia Dynamic Logistics Promises Delivered Conditional Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Got off to a winning start over hurdles over 2m here in August 2024; a remote last to finish on all her three starts since, however, latest her chasing debut at Stratford (2m4f; three ran) in May; very hard to warm to.

RaceName: Britannia Dynamic Logistics Promises Delivered Conditional Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Best effort when third in 2m6f Stratford novice in May; came in a well-held fifth in similar company at Southwell (2m4f) 22 days ago but he may still do better now going into handicaps.

RaceName: Britannia Dynamic Logistics Promises Delivered Conditional Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Resumed winning ways at Newton Abbot (3m2f) last September but below par all three subsequent runs, beating one in a 3m2f Warwick handicap four weeks ago; needs to take a big step forward.

RaceName: Britannia Dynamic Logistics Promises Delivered Conditional Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Winning pointer; gained a first success under rules on just his second run for current yard in 3m Southwell handicap hurdle 22 days ago; up 7lb but not taken lightly.

RaceName: Britannia Dynamic Logistics Promises Delivered Conditional Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: A fair 2m1f winning hurdler who wasn't disgraced when fourth on the Flat at Bath 18 days ago; this veteran is no forlorn hope back in this sphere.

RaceName: Britannia Dynamic Logistics Promises Delivered Conditional Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Gained a first handicap success at Hereford (2m4f) in April but seemed unsuited by dropping back to 2m1f when third of five in novice at Cartmel in May; this daughter of Jukebox Jury remains one to be interested in over this much longer trip.

RaceName: Britannia Dynamic Logistics Promises Delivered Conditional Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Fair maiden hurdler; failed to kick on sent handicapping when only fifth of seven at Uttoxeter (2m7f) in May; remains with few miles on the clock, though, so not written off.
Way better than this grade in his prime and he was running quite well (7f/1m) until his last race when poorly drawn; better boxed this time and Nichola Yuen keeps the faith; fourth in a recent trial.
Longstanding maiden from 5f to 1m; however, placed in two of his last three races and Purton was aboard when runner-up to a comfortable winner over this far at Happy Valley latest.

OwnerName: Alex Chau Man Lok, Jack Chan Hoi Et Al
Spotlight: Beaten a nose over 7f here in March and another good run when fourth two starts back (1m); however, he's not that consistent, ran moderately in a recent trial and is drawn in 14.
No wins in 19 but arrives in form despite having had to contend with some wide draws; good chance this time from stall 1.

OwnerName: Hong Yiu, Lynn Fu Lam Wu Et Al
Spotlight: Very well handicapped on last season's placed form and he has a kinder draw than last time; just a concern that he's perhaps better on dirt.

OwnerName: Robert Chung Wai Shu & Jeanette Chung Siu Wah
Spotlight: Made it 1-39 when beaten 4l last time at Happy Valley; the return of blinkers could be a good move, though.
His two efforts earlier in the year off higher marks than today make him a player in this company but he's finished down the field in his last two races, the latest only a week ago.
Made all over the 1m here in November but hasn't really approached that form since; others preferred from his wide stall.
Unplaced all eight starts; has been shaping as though further than 1m is worth a go (recent non-runner over 1m3f) but he's down in grade off a reduced mark and could have some role to play.
Won twice in Britain but has a poor strike-rate in Hong Kong and he was behind two of these last time.
16-race maiden who hasn't been far away in his last three races over 7f and his career-best RPR came at a mile; one to consider.
Has dropped to a good mark for a reason and needs to turn a corner; opposable, for all that he's downgraded into a Class 5.
Both wins over C&D but his recent efforts have been none too inspiring.
Both wins have come here over 7f, the latest in early 2025; has run some respectable races during his losing sequence and won a recent trial.
Hadn't even placed before springing a surprise over C&D last month; held a good position throughout that day and he's gone up 5lb; could be vulnerable.
0-18 but off a much reduced mark he finished well for a close fifth over C&D last time; has a kinder draw now; considered.
Went up 11lb for winning two races in three starts; had a tough draw the last time but he might be on a tough mark now and was well beaten in a recent trial.
Hasn't been in the best of form, he's drawn wide and probably needs further; others preferred.
No wins in 11 but was a respectable fifth here two runs ago and a slow start did for him when only 5-1 for a C&D handicap last time.
Still a maiden after 11 attempts and mostly disappointing this year; has lots to prove.
Maiden winner for Aidan O'Brien but very opposable on his Hong Kong form.
Placed over C&D in March and 1m doesn't appear to be his trip, so forgiven last run; however, subsequent trial efforts nothing to shout about.

OwnerName: Chan Wing Kwong, Patrick Ng Pak Hong Et Al
Spotlight: Both wins have come over C&D and he hasn't performed too badly of late at Happy Valley when faced with tricky draws; he's landed another one here, though Nichola Yuen's claim is a bonus.

OwnerName: Daren Wong Wai Chow, Lau Kay Jing & Eric Liu Kar Hang
Spotlight: Just 1-29 and struggles have continued in Class 5 races on last three starts; not easily fancied from stall 13.

OwnerName: 23/24 Cody Mo Wai Kit Trainer Syndicate (T1)
Spotlight: Off a career-low mark when defying a wide draw over C&D in May but couldn't defy another off this 6lb higher mark a month ago (1m); beaten but ran well in his latest trial.

OwnerName: Mr & Mrs Matthew Lam Kin Hong
Spotlight: Out of the frame in all ten starts and fared no better dropped into a Class 5 a month ago, albeit from a poor draw; has done most of his racing at sprint trips and has yet to shine over 7f.
Struggled on recent stable debut at Happy Valley and is now 0-13 in Hong Kong; needs to turn a corner.
Won in New Zealand but has a regressive profile in Hong Kong and that continued dropped into a Class 5 last time; new trainer.

RaceName: Friends Of Hong Kong Association Cup (Handicap) (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: No wins in 19 but has competitive form and was closing at the finish when drawn wide in his first Class 4 last time; respected.

RaceName: Friends Of Hong Kong Association Cup (Handicap) (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: All four runs at 6f and no better than seventh in any of them; might still be on a tough mark.

RaceName: Friends Of Hong Kong Association Cup (Handicap) (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
OwnerName: Sammy Ma Hon Kit/Chan Sin Shui/Exec Of Est Of J C T Cheung
Spotlight: All six runs for this yard highly respectable, and he clinged on over C&D ten days ago; essentially just 2lb higher if taking into account Ellis Wong's claim; clear chance.

RaceName: Friends Of Hong Kong Association Cup (Handicap) (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Both wins have been here, at 7f and 1m; had been struggling for form but ran well in a recent trial and Nichola Yuen claims 10lb.

RaceName: Friends Of Hong Kong Association Cup (Handicap) (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
OwnerName: Raymond Tsang Heung Tak & Kenneth Tsang Heung Kan
Spotlight: Hasn't been all that far away in his 6f handicaps and last two races haven't gone entirely to plan, finishing well in the latest; Purton is back on board and 7f is well worth trying.

RaceName: Friends Of Hong Kong Association Cup (Handicap) (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
OwnerName: Dr & Mrs Simon Suen Siu Man And Chloe Suen
Spotlight: He was in good form but his mark suffered and he now has to bounce back from two lesser efforts.

RaceName: Friends Of Hong Kong Association Cup (Handicap) (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: 4-4 in Brazil but 0-19 in Hong Kong despite a sliding mark; probably needs further.

RaceName: Friends Of Hong Kong Association Cup (Handicap) (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Can front run but doesn't have to; won't be inconvenienced by returning to turf but stall 11 is not ideal and he struggles for consistency.

RaceName: Friends Of Hong Kong Association Cup (Handicap) (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Hasn't been showing enough in his trials to believe he can make a successful official debut.

RaceName: Friends Of Hong Kong Association Cup (Handicap) (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
OwnerName: Ho Yin Sang & Tsang Kin Wa
Spotlight: Well beaten in his four races (6f-7f) and it's been a mixed bag in trials (won once) since his last official race.

RaceName: Friends Of Hong Kong Association Cup (Handicap) (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Has won a trial but down the field in both his official races and he's poorly drawn.

RaceName: Friends Of Hong Kong Association Cup (Handicap) (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
OwnerName: Chow Kay Yui, Kong Kin Kuen & Lau Wai Shing
Spotlight: Has finished second in a trial since two very underwhelming efforts on the track; probably best to turn elsewhere.

RaceName: Friends Of Hong Kong Association Cup (Handicap) (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Lightly raced; had shaped like a winner waiting to happen but has disappointed in his last two races and now goes further than 6f for the first time.

RaceName: Friends Of Hong Kong Association Cup (Handicap) (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)
Spotlight: Finished last on official debut over 6f here in March (118-1); has since won a trial but others preferred.
Off the mark when an impressive winner over C&D in May and Zac Purton keeps the ride; should figure, for all that he's not that well drawn and has gone up 8lb.
Only Hong Kong win came over 5f at Happy Valley where he has run many of his better races; remains in good form and would have claims if he can transfer that to this venue.
Runner-up in his latest barrier trial so has ability and a favourable draw for today's debut.
Has form figures of 63244 in this year's trials and likely best watched on debut.

OwnerName: 24/25 Dennis C H Yip Trainer Syndicate (2)
Spotlight: Clear claims on his head defeat over 6f here in March but has lost his way since; hasn't raced over 5f since debut.

Seemingly confirmed that he's happier on dirt than turf when again successful over 6f here in early May, and has run well over the same C&D since; bit to prove back on the grass (0-5) and dropped to 5f.
Finished last on official debut over C&D three months ago; unable to win any of his subsequent trials.
Won a trial before finishing runner-up from stall 14 here a month ago; the winner of that race is unexposed and this 3yo again has a useful draw to work from.

OwnerName: Cheung Tin Chai, Cheung Shu Yan & Cheung Shu Tak
Spotlight: No better than fifth in his recent trials and can only be watched on debut; tricky draw.
Often strong in the betting and usually involved in the finish, as his form figures would imply; however, all 15 races have been at 6f and 7f so it's whether he's quick enough for 5f.
C&D winner last September and some good runs since on the dirt; below par in his last two races, though.

OwnerName: 25/26 Pierre Ng Pang Chi Trainer Syndicate
Spotlight: Worrying that he took a few goes to win a trial which he did only recently; unlikely to be the answer.

OwnerName: Bernie Yim Mei Fung & Yim Man Hay
Spotlight: Runner-up in a trial but couldn't win one and had plenty of chances; best watched.

Consistent 4yo with a strike-rate of 4-17 after winning over 6f here a month ago in good style; this is a rise in grade and he's 8lb higher, but should give his running; stays 7f; second in a recent trial.

OwnerName: Eleanor Kwok Law Kwai Chun & Patrick Kwok Ho Chuen
Spotlight: 6f looked too sharp last time but he probably wants further than this far having done so well at 1m; he'll need a strong pace up front.
Gained all five wins over 6f but only eighth over that far last time after contesting the Hong Kong Derby; others preferred.
Classy form in Australia and back on track for his new yard with length defeats in 6f handicaps here; won't mind returning to 7f.

C&D winner who won over 6f here in January and has continued to run well at that trip, including when nearest at the finish in a Group 3 handicap a month ago; respected off the same mark now back over further.
One of three wins last season was a 7f Group 3 handicap; he can deal with this mark and latest run here over 6f was eyecatching.
Some feat to win four consecutive handicaps (6f-7f) between December and March but his form has run cold in races over 1m2f, 7f and 6f; even if back on song this mark isn't easy.
Nichola Yuen claims 10lb but he needs a turnaround in his form and was only sixth in a recent trial.

OwnerName: Vincent To Wai Keung, K To Kin Ting & R To Yiu Ting
Spotlight: 3-14; perhaps unlucky not to win 1m Class 2 handicap here two runs ago and following defeat was over 1m2f; has to prove himself worthy of this high a mark but not dismissed; recent trial figures of 643 are not overly inspiring, however.

Three-time C&D winner who is comfortable at this level; highly tried behind Ka Ying Rising before struggling from a bad draw in a handicap here two months ago; only fifth and fourth in subsequent trials but dangerous to dismiss.
Seemingly back in good nick when 48-1 second over C&D in May but was a disappointing favourite here last month and has another double-digit draw today; others look safer.
Unlucky not to finish closer than seventh on debut in May and kept on well for close third later that month; moves up from 5f today and could still have a lot more to offer; commands respect.

OwnerName: Mr & Mrs Colin Lo Chor Cheong
Spotlight: Makes official debut after some underwhelming recent trials; favourable draw not enough to make him of interest.
Persistently denied clear run on debut here (5f) in February but posed no threat the following month; upped in trip for first run since; probably one for a bit further down the line.
Slightly disappointing on dirt here three months ago and not seen since but was placed on his latest turf start and has slipped to a tempting mark; can't be ruled out.
11-race maiden; finished well for close fifth in 7f Class 5 event here ten days ago but is quickly back up in grade today and the drop back to 6f is not an obvious move.
Ran on well for third over C&D two starts ago but 7f is his optimum trip, and he disappointed when last in action; others preferred.
Held on today's mark twice since winning readily from the front over C&D in early May but is better drawn this time and could give this a good shot.
A well-beaten outsider on both starts (6f/7f); can't be recommended.

Began career with two promising 6f runs at Happy Valley, keeping on well to make the frame both times; will probably be dropped in again from his wide draw here; no surprise if he appears late on the scene.
Out of a 6.5f winner; came second in a recent 6f dirt trial and evidently has some ability; makes official debut today.

OwnerName: Dr Bankee Kwan Pak Hoo & Jeffrey Kwan Teng Hin
Spotlight: Began season with two down-the-field runs in the autumn and returned from a break with another low-key effort last month; drawn wide; not the most obvious answer.

OwnerName: Chan Tim Shing & Pang Wai Hak
Spotlight: Three wins this year, the latest over C&D in May; held off this mark since, though, and needs to kick on again.

OwnerName: Yeung Wai Kwong & Lee Sheung Ping
Spotlight: 0-8 in Hong Kong but placed here on last four appearances, losing out only by a short head on latest occasion 24 days ago; ought to be right in the thick of things again.

OwnerName: Hong Kong St John Presidents Club Syndicate
Spotlight: Doubled Hong Kong tally over C&D in Apri, holding on by a neck under 10lb claimer Nichola Yuen, but was safely held on his latest outing and needs to rebound with a career-best effort.
On fairy tough mark since narrow 6f win in March but recent 5f form has been respectable (cheekpieces refitted last time) and he's well drawn; probably won't be far away.

OwnerName: Dr Alan Lee Wai Kin, Dr Kelvin Chong Man Yuk Et Al
Spotlight: Left Manfred Man after a string of disappointing efforts and there has been no upturn after three starts for current stable; difficult to enthuse over.

OwnerName: Edmund Ho Kwok Shing, Joe Fong Fu Yuen Et Al
Spotlight: Exploited reduced in C&D Class 4 event in April but didn't run well here last month and his overall profile is not that appealing.
Drops back to optimum trip after three down-the-field finishes over 6f/7f but is still 4lb higher than when forcing a dead-heat over C&D in March; others are more persuasive.

OwnerName: 25/26 Dennis C H Yip Trainer Syndicate (2)
Spotlight: 0-7 in Hong Kong but has been making good late headway on recent outings; each-way claims at least if he gets a good pace to aim at.
Gained all five wins over 7f, the latest on seasonal debut in September; not disgraced over C&D when dropped back in trip two starts ago but generally in poor form since December; look elsewhere.
Runner-up twice over 6f here in February/March and quite well handicapped on that form but hasn't been at the same level lately and needs to prove he's fully effective over 5f.
Listed 2yo winner when based in Italy but struggled on Hong Kong debut here (6f) one month ago.
Low-mileage 5yo who won twice in Class 4 last year (5f/6f); returned from layoff with low-key run last month but remains open to progress if that outing brought him on.
Has a C&D win on his CV but is better known for his exploits at Happy Valley; out of form this season and needs a revival.
Ex-Australian 3yo; well beaten over C&D on Hong Kong debut in May and needs to leave that performance a long way behind.

Scored over C&D for Cody Mo in December and was placed over C&D in cheekpieces on final two starts for that stable; goes without headgear and has wide draw on debut for Danny Shum but booking of Zac Purton catches the eye.
Unexposed 3yo who made quite promising debut when keeping-on sixth here (6f) in May; no surprise if he gets a bit closer to the target over this longer trip.
Slightly disappointing when last in action and not one to rely upon heavily but has posted some good efforts this season (including a C&D win) and his current mark is not beyond him.
Unplaced all five starts (6f-7f); certainly not devoid of ability but has awkward draw today and will probably be of greater interesting when eligible for Class 5 races.

OwnerName: Yip Yuk Kit & Dr Tommy Yip Chun Ming
Spotlight: Came good at eighth attempt, with front-running C&D win last month; well drawn to attack again and might use that success as a springboard.

Yet to make any significant further progress since winning over 6f here on debut in March but kept on for third over C&D when upped in trip last month and has each-way claims again, despite his much tougher draw.
Well down the field on first four starts but his keeping-on fifth here (6f) last month was a step in the right direction; back up in trip today.
Made low-key debut when a 40-1 outsider over C&D this month; remains best watched.

OwnerName: Albert Hung Chao Hong & Kevin Hung Dingteng
Spotlight: 0-17 in Hong Kong but runner-up twice over C&D in recent weeks and nicely drawn on the inside today; big player if first-time blinkers give him a boost.
Stays 7f but is much better known as a sprinter; without a win and has very mixed record this season; not the percentage call.

OwnerName: Jason Cheung Cho Yee & Cheung Ka Shing
Spotlight: Consistent 6yo who has collected two C&D wins this season; likely to give another good account but has tough draw and career-high mark to contend with.
Showed only minor promise on his two 6f starts and needs this extra furlong to unlock improvement.
Always behind when a 56-1 outsider for debut here (6f) last month.
Badly out of form over 6f in recent months and this 59th career start with be his first venture beyond that trip.
A maiden after seven runs but kept on well to take fourth from stall 14 over C&D in May and has now made the frame on his last three outings; ought to be on the premises again.
Unplaced over 7f on first three starts but evidently well suited by step up in trip when close second over C&D in May; drawn wide today but has scope for further progress.
Has modest strike-rate and is no longer the force of old but made the frame over C&D on his last two outings and continues to edge down the weights; Zac Purton rides today; obvious each-way claims.

OwnerName: Raymond Tsang Heung Tak & Kenneth Tsang Heung Kan
Spotlight: Scored over C&D in February and emerged from a dip in form when close fifth here last month; still on a workable mark; a possible.
Unexposed 3yo; launched career with two encouraging runs here in May (7f/1m) but needs to take a step forward today.
Runner-up in higher-grade company on seasonal debut in September (7f) but generally very disappointing since; needs to turn things around.

OwnerName: Wilson Chan Wai Kwok, Joseph Yim Wai Ho & Gary Ng Ka Wai
Spotlight: Dual Class 5 winner (once over C&D) for new stable in early part of this season but has found things tougher in this grade since the turn of the year.

OwnerName: Tony Stradmoor, Billy Li Sze Kuen Et Al
Spotlight: Three Class 5 wins at Happy Valley this season, the latest four weeks ago; evidently in good nick but combination of latest 6lb rise and a rise in grade might leave him vulnerable.
Opened account over C&D in March and ran at least as well when second here the following month; not quite in same form lately, though, and has awkward draw today.
Just held on to score in refitted blinkers over C&D last month and is now 2-4 for current stable; might still be on the upgrade; shortlisted.
Well down the field when upped to 1m2f here recently and has now been unplaced all four Hong Kong starts; drops in grade today but still makes only limited appeal.
1-24 in Hong Kong, the win gained at Happy Valley (1m) in March; still 3lb above that mark and needs to up his game after some below-par efforts.
Came good at fourth attempt, with narrow 7f course win in March, but form has nosedived since; moves up in trip with a lot to prove.
Overcame wide draw and some traffic to score over 7f here last month, his 12th start; went up 7lb for that but could quite plausibly have untapped potential over this new trip.
Began this year with two course wins (7f/1m); still in good form and has better draw than last time but needs to find hidden reserves to overcome current mark.

OwnerName: Thomas Liang Cheung Biu & Elizabeth Lee Wong Sing Fung
Spotlight: Progressive 4yo who scored over C&D when upped in trip in April and has made the frame over C&D on both starts since; may yet have a bigger effort in him; respected.

OwnerName: Mr & Mrs Stephen Ho Wai Man, F Ho Ka Chun & D Ho Ka Chung
Spotlight: Quite useful when trained in Britain and has showed promise on three of his four Hong Kong starts, most recently when keeping-on sixth over C&D in May; another 2lb lower today; could have a say.
Dual dirt winner this year; managed only sixth off this career-high mark over C&D in May and has much wider draw today but Nichola Yuen's 10lb claim will be helpful.

OwnerName: Dr & Mrs Henry H L Chan
Spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo who won by a short head on consecutive appearances here in January/February (1m/1m1f); this hat-trick attempt comes after a break but further progress is possible.

OwnerName: Thomas Chung Yat Fan & Joe Ho Pui Lung
Spotlight: Placed twice since cheekpieces were refitted in May, more recently when running-on third at Happy Valley (1m); remains well handicapped and will be a tough nut to crack if switch to first-time visor has any positive effect.

Caused 46-1 surprise over C&D in February, his second Hong Kong start, and finished well to make the frame on last couple of outings; can be dangerous under Zac Purton if he gets a strong pace to aim at.

OwnerName: Friend Of Yuen Long Merchants Assn Syndicate
Spotlight: Won twice (once over C&D) last season but has gone the wrong way since an encouraging reappearance run in September; others have much less to prove.
Sixth at big odds over C&D in April but quickly back in the doldrums on next two outings and has it all to prove again.
Won on dirt in early part of this season but has been badly out of form on turf in recent months; needs to turn things around.

OwnerName: Zhao Guoming, Xiao Bing, Annie An Ran & Tan Meng Sha
Spotlight: Belied huge odds when fourth over C&D in April, his second Hong Kong start, but was unable to land a telling blow here last month; swaps cheekpieces for blinkers today.

OwnerName: David Philip Boehm & Anthony Cheung Hin Shun
Spotlight: Signed off in Ireland with two 7f wins in April 2025 but has been soundly beaten on all three Hong Kong starts this season (7f-1m).
Won off much-reduced mark at Happy Valley (6f) in February and is also suited by this C&D but recent efforts have been underwhelming and younger rivals have much more appealing profiles.

OwnerName: Yue Yun Hing & Anita Yue Lam Chik Sun
Spotlight: Won 7f maiden on second of his two appearances in Brazil but struggled on first two Hong Kong starts (both C&D).

Improving 4yo who has collected three 6f wins (one here) this season and made good late headway to take third off today's mark at Happy Valley last month; probably needs to take another step forward today, though.

OwnerName: Mr & Mrs Chan Hou Bo & Chan Jenny Chun Lai
Spotlight: Dual 5f course winner last year; made nothing better than a satisfactory reappearance over that trip last month but remains unexposed over 6f and lightly raced in general; contender if that recent outing brought him on.

OwnerName: 25/26 Frankie Lor Fu Chuen Trainer Syndicate (2)
Spotlight: Scored by a neck over C&D on debut in May and ran well in defeat here last month; probably still has potential; high on the list.
Made quite promising Hong Kong debut when fourth over C&D in February but we haven't seen him since a lesser effort the following month and he's poorly drawn today.
Won over 5f in Australia last year and made promising Hong Kong debut when third over that trip here last month; drawn wide for first 6f attempt but still enters calculations.

OwnerName: Dr Edwin Lam Chi Wan & Jason Lam Che Man
Spotlight: Generally progressive 4yo who posted a good effort when placed behind Tycoon Resources at Happy Valley last month, after a break; competes off same mark here but has much tougher draw this time.
Won twice in lower-grade company at Happy Valley (6f/5f) in early part of this season but has lost his way a bit since; others in this line-up have much stronger claims.
Yet to make a splash after four runs in Hong Kong and seemingly in need of a drop in grade.

OwnerName: HK Association Of Lady Horse Lovers Syndicate
Spotlight: Not at his best this year but handicapped accordingly now and was close up from a double-digit draw over C&D last month; can make his presence felt again.
Three-time C&D winner; much more exposed than some of his rivals but has been back in good form in recent months and Nichola Yuen's 10lb claim gives him some wiggle room; considered each-way.
Smart 2yo when based in Ireland but a down-the-field outsider on all six Hong Kong starts.
Exciting 3yo who won comfortably when upped to 6f at Happy Valley last month and is now 3-3; another 9lb higher for first run here but it's hard to gauge the limit of his ability.
